notes:
Done what others have said, by renaming starting file and editing configuration file. I haven't installed fps unblocker though. Game played stable, sometimes it slowed down, but it's due that fps unblocker I presume.
I've used this tutorial:
To launch the game: go to ~/.steam/Steam/steamapps/common/Star\ Wars\ The\ Force\ Unleashed/ remove the SWTFU Launcher.exe file rename the SWTFU.exe file to SWTFU Launcher.exe 
To change the screen resolution(after launching game first): go to ~/.steam/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/32430/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local\ Settings/Application\ Data/Aspyr/Star\ Wars\ The\ Force\ Unleashed/ edit the Config.xml file 
To unlock 60fps: download https://community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/506-star-wars-the-force-unleashed-12-fps-cap-fix/ extract it place the SWTFU.exe file from the extracted Star Wars - The Force Unleashed 60 FPS folder in ~/.steam/Steam/steamapps/common/Star\ Wars\ The\ Force\ Unleashed/ go to ~/.steam/Steam/steamapps/common/Star\ Wars\ The\ Force\ Unleashed/ remove the SWTFU Launcher.exe file rename the SWTFU.exe file to SWTFU Launcher.exe It tries to install the Microsoft .NET Framework on every launch, ignore it, it takes just some seconds
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition

notes: Game does not launch. Status on Steam briefly says that I'm playing the game, but then within a second reverts back to online and no game appears.
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition

notes: Applied the tweaks others have described here and it ran fine through Steam Link with a Steam Controller on my tv. A few glitches sometimes (mostly with music in one level), but that seems to be due to the game itself, not Proton. Finished the whole game.
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition

notes:
Basically, I followed a previous suggested things, which I shall now rehash. And honestly, it runs better than on Windows. | 


 Brief Note: Under a default Lin Steam installation, all of the following folders will be on in your ~/.steam folder, in your home drive. I, however, have them on a separate HDD, so I am conveying it differently. | 


 Prenote: I would recommend going to the LutrisWiki on Github and go through their tutorials installing any and all graphics drivers if new || 



 Step 1. Get rid of that useless launcher


(1.1) go to /... /steamapps/common/Star\ Wars\ The\ Force\ Unleashed/
(1.2) Delete "SWTFU Launcher.exe"
(1.3) Rename "SWTFU.exe" to "SWTFU Launcher.exe || 


Step 2. Edit the conf file because the defaults are garbage


(2.1) Launch the game just in case, to generate the conf file if, by chance, it was not there by default, then close it
(2.2) go to: /... /steamapps/compatdata/32430/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local\ Settings/Application\ Data/Aspyr/Star\ Wars\ The\ Force\ Unleashed/
(2.3) do edit the config.xml as an administrator, or replace yours with mine, as unless you use a controller, the default controls are rather obnoxious and I would have made them better if I understood xml better, if able: 
§§ https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1wq-LaqyuehpMwvR_-2lQsKRDtGs95IIu?usp=sharing §§
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition
STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition

notes: The game doesn't work out of the box on Proton. To get it working, I had to rename the launcher to something else and then rename to game exe file to the original launcher name. Then the game worked fine, only the resolution cannot be increased in game. You need to edit the config file directly the the application settings. Then the game worked even in 4k. I installed D9VK, but then the game showed only a blackscreen, so you have to use WineD3D.
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition

notes:
I got the game running by changing the launch options to [DO=(%command%); "${DO[@]/%SWTFU Launcher.exe/SWTFU.exe}"] (Square brackets excluded) this was taken from the Borderlands Remastered page and tweaked for this game. The resolution does need to be manually set and the config file can be located at [~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/32430/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/Aspyr/Star Wars The Force Unleashed/Config.xml]. 

After finding the above, I have had no issues on an otherwise default build
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition

notes: Following previously posted guides I was able to get the game running, played through first mission as vader, but the game always crashes before the start of the mission on nar shadda. Sometimes when launched the game gets past the intro then into a black screen. Restarting usually fixes but I cant progress any further in the game because of the crash.
title: STAR WARS™ - The Force Unleashed™ Ultimate Sith Edition
